data_collected.source_quote: Lyft collects information about your use of the Lyft Platform including ride information like date, time, destination, distance, route, payment. Device information includes device model, IP address, browser type, advertising identifiers, and device identifiers.

purposes_of_collection.source_quote: Lyft collects tracking data to understand how users navigate the platform and interact with advertisements, improve safety, learn what content is popular, and serve better ads.

shared_for_advertising.source_quote: Lyft may share personal information with third parties to deliver personalized ads on and off the Lyft Platform.

cross_site_tracking.source_quote: Lyft collects information through cookies, tracking pixels, data analytics tools, SDKs, and third-party tracking technologies.

used_for_profiling.source_quote: Lyft uses data to serve targeted advertising and personalized content.

third_party_recipients.source_quote: Lyft shares personal information with third parties to deliver personalized ads and may cross-verify data with third-party service providers.

opt_out_of_sale_sharing.source_quote: Users may opt out of 'selling,' 'sharing,' or targeted advertising by navigating to Privacy Settings in the Lyft app.

encrypted_in_transit.source_quote: Lyft uses secure protocols including HTTPS (TLS 1.2) and SFTP for data transmission.

encrypted_at_rest.source_quote: Databases containing user information are encrypted at rest.

independent_audits.source_quote: Lyft performs regular internal and third-party external infrastructure scans and code base scans with a bug bounty program.

gaps: access and delete rights not mentioned
GPC and DNT signal handling not mentioned
sold to third parties not clearly stated
label_notes: Lyft collects extensive data including location, biometric, and device information for service provision and advertising. Strong tracking and profiling with many third-party sharing arrangements. Encryption in transit and at rest is confirmed. User can opt out of targeted advertising through app settings. Uses biometric imagery for identity verification. Lacks transparency on data retention and many consumer rights.
